description: TypeScript port of the fitzRoy R package as a pure library for AFL data access

userStories.title: Project restructure and dependency setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project restructured from a Worker app to a library with the correct directory layout and dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove hono and drizzle-orm from dependencies; add cheerio as a dependency
Create src/index.ts as the package entry point (empty re-exports for now)
Create directory structure: src/sources/, src/transforms/, src/api/, src/lib/
Create src/lib/result.ts with Result<T, E> type and ok()/err() helpers
Create src/lib/errors.ts with AflApiError, ScrapeError, and ValidationError custom error classes
Update package.json to remove worker scripts (dev, deploy) and add exports/main field pointing to src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Restructured from Worker to library. Removed hono, drizzle-orm, wrangler, cloudflare-workers-types. Added cheerio. Created src/sources/, src/transforms/, src/api/, src/lib/result.ts, src/lib/errors.ts, src/index.ts.

userStories.title: Domain types and type aliases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all shared domain types defined so that implementations can reference them.
acceptanceCriteria: Define MatchResult interface in src/types.ts with all match fields (teams, scores, venue, date, per-quarter scores)
Define PlayerStats interface with all player stat fields (disposals, kicks, marks, etc.)
Define Fixture, Lineup, Ladder, Squad, Team interfaces
Define CompetitionCode union type ('AFLM' | 'AFLW' | etc.), RoundType union, DataSource union ('afl-api' | 'footywire' | 'afl-tables')
Define common query parameter types (SeasonRoundQuery, MatchQuery, etc.)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Defined all domain types in src/types.ts: MatchResult, PlayerStats, Fixture, Lineup, Ladder, Squad, Team, plus union types (CompetitionCode, RoundType, DataSource, MatchStatus) and query parameter types (SeasonRoundQuery, MatchQuery, PlayerStatsQuery, LineupQuery, LadderQuery, TeamQuery, SquadQuery). Re-exported all types from src/index.ts.

userStories.title: Team name mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a comprehensive team name mapping so that team names are normalised across all data sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/lib/team-mapping.ts with a canonical team name lookup covering all AFL/VFL teams
Map all known variants: abbreviations (GWS), full names (Greater Western Sydney Giants), historical names (Footscray -> Western Bulldogs)
Export normaliseTeamName(raw: string): string function that returns the canonical name
Handle case-insensitive lookups
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented canonical team name lookup with case-insensitive matching. Covers all 18 current AFL teams plus historical/defunct VFL teams (Fitzroy, University). Maps abbreviations, full names with mascots, mascot-only names, and historical names (Footscray, South Melbourne, Brisbane Bears). Exported normaliseTeamName from index.ts.

userStories.title: Date utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need date parsing and AEST/AEDT handling utilities for normalising dates from all sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/lib/date-utils.ts with parseAflApiDate() for UTC ISO strings from the AFL API
Implement parseFootyWireDate() for FootyWire date formats
Implement parseAflTablesDate() for AFL Tables date formats
Implement toAestString() to convert a Date to an AEST/AEDT-aware display string
All functions use only Web Standard APIs (Date, Intl.DateTimeFormat)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented parseAflApiDate (UTC ISO strings), parseFootyWireDate (DD Mon YYYY variants), parseAflTablesDate (DD-Mon-YYYY variants), and toAestString (Intl.DateTimeFormat with Australia/Melbourne). All use only Web Standard APIs. Returns null for invalid inputs.

userStories.title: Zod validation schemas for AFL API responses
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Zod schemas to validate all AFL API response shapes at the boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/validation.ts with Zod schemas for AFL API token response
Add schemas for competition list, compseason list, and round list responses
Add schemas for match items (round results), single match detail, and player stats responses
Add schemas for match roster, team list, and squad responses
Export inferred TypeScript types from each schema (z.infer<typeof Schema>)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AFL API authentication and base client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an AFL API client that handles token authentication and provides typed fetch helpers.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/sources/afl-api.ts with AflApiClient class that accepts a fetch function (for testability)
Implement authenticate() that calls the WMCTok token endpoint and caches the token
Implement authedFetch() that adds the bearer token and retries once on 401 (re-authenticating)
Implement fetchJson<T>(url, schema) helper that fetches, parses JSON, and validates with Zod, returning Result<T>
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AFL API metadata resolution (competitions, seasons, rounds)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to resolve competition IDs, season IDs, and round IDs from human-readable parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: Add resolveCompetitionId(code: CompetitionCode) to AflApiClient returning Result<string>
Add resolveSeasonId(competitionId: string, year: number) returning Result<string>
Add resolveRounds(seasonId: string) returning Result<Round[]> with round metadata
Add resolveRoundId(seasonId: string, roundNumber: number) returning Result<string>
Each method uses the Zod-validated fetchJson helper from US-006
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AFL API match results and match detail fetching
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fetch match results for a round and detailed match info from the AFL API.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fetchRoundMatchItems(roundId: string) to AflApiClient returning Result<RawMatchItem[]>
Add fetchMatchDetail(matchProviderId: string) returning Result<RawMatchDetail>
Add fetchSeasonMatchItems(seasonId: string) that iterates completed rounds and aggregates results
All responses validated through Zod schemas before returning
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AFL API player stats, lineups, and squads
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fetch player statistics, match rosters, and team squads from the AFL API.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fetchPlayerStats(matchProviderId: string) to AflApiClient returning Result<RawPlayerStats[]>
Add fetchMatchRoster(matchProviderId: string) returning Result<RawMatchRoster>
Add fetchTeams(options?: { teamType?: string }) returning Result<RawTeam[]>
Add fetchSquad(teamId: string, seasonId: string) returning Result<RawSquadPlayer[]>
All responses validated through Zod schemas
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Match results transform
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pure transform functions to flatten and normalise raw AFL API match data into typed MatchResult objects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/transforms/match-results.ts with transformMatchItems(raw: RawMatchItem[]): MatchResult[]
Flatten nested periodScore arrays into per-quarter columns (q1Home, q1Away, etc.)
Normalise team names using team-mapping
Parse and normalise dates using date-utils
No I/O or side effects in this module
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Player stats transform
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pure transform functions to flatten and normalise raw AFL API player stats into typed PlayerStats objects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/transforms/player-stats.ts with transformPlayerStats(raw: RawPlayerStats[]): PlayerStats[]
Strip playerStats. and playerName. prefixes from field names
Normalise team names using team-mapping
Handle missing or null stat values gracefully (default to 0 or undefined as appropriate)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Lineup transform
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pure transform functions to normalise raw AFL API roster data into typed Lineup objects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/transforms/lineup.ts with transformMatchRoster(raw: RawMatchRoster): Lineup
Normalise team names and player positions
Handle interchange, emergency, and substitute designations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: FootyWire scraper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a FootyWire client to scrape match results and player stats as a fallback source.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sources/footywire.ts with fetchSeasonResults(year: number) returning Result<MatchResult[]>
Implement fetchMatchPlayerStats(matchId: string) returning Result<PlayerStats[]>
Use cheerio to parse HTML responses
Set appropriate request headers to handle Cloudflare protection
Normalise team names and dates using lib utilities
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AFL Tables scraper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an AFL Tables client to scrape historical season results and player data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sources/afl-tables.ts with fetchSeasonResults(year: number) returning Result<MatchResult[]>
Support seasons from 1897 to present
Implement fetchPlayerDetails(playerUrl: string) returning Result<PlayerBio> with height, weight, DOB
Use cheerio to parse HTML responses
Normalise team names and dates using lib utilities
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fryzigg client stub or implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Fryzigg source to either fetch advanced stats or be explicitly documented as unsupported.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sources/fryzigg.ts
Investigate whether fryziggafl.net exposes JSON/CSV endpoints
If endpoints exist: implement fetchAdvancedStats() with Zod validation
If no endpoints exist: export a stub function that returns an err() Result with a clear explanation (R-specific RDS binary format)
Document the decision in a TSDoc comment on the module
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Public API: fetchMatchResults
userStories.description: As a TypeScript developer, I want to call fetchMatchResults() with a source, season, and optional round so that I get typed match result data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/match-results.ts with fetchMatchResults({ source, season, round?, competition? }): Promise<Result<MatchResult[]>>
Route to AFL API, FootyWire, or AFL Tables based on source parameter
For AFL API source: resolve IDs, fetch raw data, transform, and return
Re-export from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Public API: fetchPlayerStats
userStories.description: As a TypeScript developer, I want to call fetchPlayerStats() to get per-player match statistics.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/player-stats.ts with fetchPlayerStats({ source, season, round?, matchId? }): Promise<Result<PlayerStats[]>>
Route to AFL API or FootyWire based on source parameter
For AFL API: resolve match IDs from season/round, fetch and transform stats
Re-export from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Public API: fetchFixture, fetchLineup, fetchLadder
userStories.description: As a TypeScript developer, I want to fetch fixture schedules, match lineups, and ladder standings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/fixture.ts with fetchFixture({ season, round?, competition? }): Promise<Result<Fixture[]>>
Create src/api/lineup.ts with fetchLineup({ season, round, matchId? }): Promise<Result<Lineup>>
Create src/api/ladder.ts with fetchLadder({ season, round?, competition? }): Promise<Result<Ladder>>
Re-export all from src/index.ts

userStories.title: Public API: fetchTeams and fetchSquad
userStories.description: As a TypeScript developer, I want to fetch team lists and squad rosters.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/teams.ts with fetchTeams({ competition?, teamType? }): Promise<Result<Team[]>>
Add fetchSquad({ teamId, season }): Promise<Result<Squad>>
Re-export from src/index.ts
Export all public types and Zod schemas from src/index.ts

userStories.title: Zod schemas for scraped HTML sources
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Zod schemas to validate data parsed from FootyWire and AFL Tables HTML.

Add Zod schemas for AFL Tables season result and player bio shapes

userStories.title: Transform tests with fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need fixture-based tests for all transform functions to ensure data normalisation is correct.

Write tests in test/sources/footywire.test.ts and test/sources/afl-tables.test.ts using HTML fixtures

Write tests with invalid/missing fields (should fail with descriptive errors)
Write tests for scrape source schemas similarly

userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive tests for team name normalisation and date parsing utilities.

Test each date parsing function with representative inputs from each source
Test toAestString produces correct AEST/AEDT output

userStories.title: Public API integration tests
